assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response explains spot instances can be preempted during upgrade process, creating 'double disruption' scenario where nodes may be lost due to both upgrades and spot preemption

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention that workloads on spot nodes are typically designed for interruption, which would make them well-suited for upgrade disruptions

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends higher surge settings with example showing maxSurge: 3 and notes 'Higher than usual'

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends maxUnavailable: 0 to 'maintain capacity', which is opposite of the expected advice to set it >0 for spot pools

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response mentions ensuring sufficient spot capacity is available during upgrade and notes backup instance types should be configured in case preferred spot types aren't available

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response provides PDB example and recommends being 'more conservative' with minAvailable settings

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides gcloud commands for upgrading node pools and updating autoscaling, but does not provide specific gcloud command for configuring surge settings

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response suggests the opposite - upgrading on-demand nodes first, with example command showing upgrade of on-demand pool first
